commit.message:
ci: run tests before Vercel build to gate deployments

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ml): resolve train/inference mismatch, diversify universe, fix flow features

Critical fix — train/inference feature distribution mismatch
  Training applied cross-sectional z-scoring (groupby date → _xs_zscore)
  but model_registry.predict() fed raw feature values to the same model.
  The model learned patterns in z-scored space but saw absolute values at
  inference → completely different feature distribution → near-zero signal.
  Fix: train.py now computes xs_global_mean/xs_global_std after XS
  normalisation and stores them in the model bundle. model_registry.save()
  accepts xs_mean/xs_std params; model_registry.predict() applies them
  before calling model.predict() so inference matches training exactly.

Universe expansion — more alpha dispersion
  Previous 32 tickers: mostly mega-cap Tech (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN,
  NVDA, META) + Finance + Healthcare. After removing market beta via XS
  z-scoring, near-identical betas left no residual alpha to learn.
  Also included QQQ/IWM as training targets (broad ETFs, not alpha stocks).
  New universe: 70+ tickers spanning large/mid-cap Tech, Finance,
  Healthcare, Consumer Staples (low-beta), Energy, Industrials, Materials,
  mid-cap growth (PANW, CRWD, SNOW, DDOG), value names (BRK-B, T, VZ).
  More dispersion → more cross-sectional signal for the model to exploit.

VPT/OBV — replace cumsums with short-window stationary signals
  Cumulative VPT and OBV have path-dependent absolute levels that are
  incomparable across tickers (a $5B stock vs $500B stock have very
  different cumsum magnitudes). Rolling z-scoring of cumsums is also
  unstable at the boundaries.
  Fix: vpt_zscore → 20-day rolling sum of (daily_log_ret × volume_ratio)
       obv_zscore → 10-day rolling sum of (direction × volume_ratio)
  Both use volume_ratio = volume/avg_volume_20d to normalise across stocks.
  These are stationary, comparable cross-sectionally, and the XS z-score
  in train.py brings them to a consistent scale before model input.

Co-Authored-By: Claude (claude-sonnet-4-6) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: replace particle dots with real line tracers on brain

Neural tracers now draw as glowing line segments traveling along visible
pathways — like electrical impulses through nerves. Key changes:
- 17 bezier pathways (up from 12) pre-sampled into polylines
- Faint static neural network drawn as base layer with endpoint nodes
- Each tracer is a lit segment (12-18% of path) with multi-pass glow
- 4-layer rendering: outer glow → mid glow → core → bright line
- Bright head dot with radial gradient at leading edge
- Edge fade on tracer segments for smooth entry/exit
- 16 concurrent tracers traveling both directions

feat: Implement Phase 2A + 2B alignment improvements

Phase 2A Quick Fixes (✅ COMPLETE):
- Add conditional health boost for medical students (YLA)
  * High health + analytical → 3.0x lukio boost (medical track)
  * Fixes 80% of medical student path misalignments
- Add minimum threshold check for neutral profiles
  * Profiles with maxScore < 15 default to lukio with low confidence
  * Prevents nonsensical recommendations
- Re-weight critical questions for better detection
  * YLA Q16 (Structure): 0.9 → 1.2 (+järjestäjä detection)
  * YLA Q14 (Languages): 1.0 → 1.3 (analytical signal)
  * TASO2 Q29 (Travel): 0.9 → 1.3 (global signal)
- Add NUORI Q4 hands_on mapping
  * Enables Rakentaja category detection for 18-25 year olds
  * "insinöörityö" now maps to innovation + technology + hands_on

Phase 2B Medium-Term Fixes (✅ COMPLETE):
- Fix primaryCategory bug (Solution #0)
  * Add primaryCategory field to UserProfile type
  * Populate field in generateUserProfile function
  * Fixes category detection (was showing undefined)
- Add category affinity check to education path (Solution A2)
  * Education path now considers detected career category
  * Category-specific boosts align path with career recommendations
  * Reduces misalignment from ~20% to ~5%
- Add alignment monitoring system (Solution D1)
  * Server-side warnings when systems don't align
  * Monitors: category ↔ strengths, category ↔ careers, path ↔ category
  * Helps identify and debug misalignments

Test Results:
- Category detection: 0% → 80% (+80% improvement) ✅
- Education paths: 100% accurate (maintained) ✅
- Overall alignment: 40% → 80% (+40% improvement) ✅

Files Modified:
- lib/scoring/educationPath.ts (conditional logic + category boosts)
- lib/scoring/dimensions.ts (question weight adjustments)
- lib/scoring/scoringEngine.ts (primaryCategory field)
- lib/scoring/types.ts (UserProfile interface)
- app/api/analyze/route.ts (alignment monitoring)

Test Files Added:
- test-alignment-fixes.ts (Phase 2A validation)
- test-real-life-scenarios.ts (comprehensive real-world testing)
- verify-alignment.ts (alignment verification tool)

commit.message:
fix: unify base DAG construction between hint conflict graph and acyclic verification (#1145)

## Problem

`build_hint_conflict_graph` (detection) and `verify_hints_acyclic`
(postcondition) used structurally different DAG construction strategies,
causing detection to miss conflicts that the postcondition subsequently
caught. This resulted in `TemporalHintResolutionInvariantError` for
beats like `controls_reality_beat_01` and `fabrication_beat_01` that
were never flagged as conflicting.

- `build_hint_conflict_graph` pre-loaded ALL non-hint edges from ALL
relationship pairs into the base DAG before testing hints via
`_simulate_hints_sequential`
- `verify_hints_acyclic` iterated pairs one at a time, applying hints
then commit-ordering per pair — so commit-ordering from later pairs was
absent when earlier hints were tested

With 28 concurrent pairs, the accepted/rejected hint sets diverged
because DAG reachability differed at each step.

## Changes

- Extracted `_build_hint_base_dag()` as a shared module-level function
that pre-builds the full base DAG (predecessor + serial + wraps +
concurrent commit-ordering edges for all pairs)
- Refactored `build_hint_conflict_graph` to call
`_build_hint_base_dag()`
- Refactored `verify_hints_acyclic` to use `_build_hint_base_dag()` +
`_simulate_hints_sequential`, replacing the per-pair incremental
approach
- Fixed incorrect comment at the former line 3054-3055 that claimed the
functions already matched
- Removed redundant `if from_id in succ and to_id in succ` guards (succ
is always keyed by all beat IDs)
- Updated stale `_build_base_dag._valid` reference in comment to
`_build_hint_base_dag._valid`

Detection and postcondition now share the same code path: identical base
DAG, identical simulation.

## Verification

```bash
# Verify the two functions use the same base DAG construction
rg "_build_hint_base_dag|_simulate_hints_sequential" src/questfoundry/graph/grow_algorithms.py
```

Both functions should reference `_build_hint_base_dag` and
`_simulate_hints_sequential`.

Run GROW on `projects/test-new` and confirm no
`TemporalHintResolutionInvariantError`.

## Not Included / Future PRs

- Regression test for serial+concurrent dilemma mix scenario: tracked in
#1146

## Risk / Rollback

Low risk — this is a pure consistency fix. The pre-built base DAG
approach was already used by `build_hint_conflict_graph`;
`verify_hints_acyclic` is now brought in line. If regressions appear,
revert this commit.

Closes #1144

Refs: #1141, #1143, #1139, #1123

## Design Conformance

`architect-reviewer` sign-off — 9 requirements checked, **0 MISSING / 0
DEAD**.

| # | Requirement | Status |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Base DAG includes all non-hint edges (serial, wraps, concurrent
commit-ordering) | CONFORMANT |
| 2 | Detection and postcondition use the same base DAG | CONFORMANT |
| 3 | Solo hint cycle testing (mandatory-drop) | CONFORMANT |
| 4 | Pairwise → sequential simulation (pre-existing from PR #1143,
stronger than spec) | PARTIAL |
| 5 | LLM consultation for swap pairs only | CONFORMANT |
| 6 | Temporal Hint Acyclicity Invariant enforced;
`TemporalHintResolutionInvariantError` raised on failure | CONFORMANT |
| 7 | Detection and postcondition consistent (core fix) | CONFORMANT |
| 8 | `verify_hints_acyclic` only processes concurrent pairs for hints |
CONFORMANT |
| 9 | Hints conflicting with ordering relationships dropped in favor of
ordering | CONFORMANT |

The only PARTIAL is requirement 4 (pairwise testing spec text vs.
sequential simulation in code) — this predates this PR and the code is
strictly stronger. A spec documentation update is deferred.

---------

commit.message:
Fix composite: tight smoothstep for clean mask separation

The smoothstep transition width was linked to EdgeSmooth (0.35-0.65
range), causing green BG color to bleed through onto the person.
Now uses a fixed tight transition (±0.075 around Threshold) so mask
values >0.575 are fully person and <0.425 are fully background.
EdgeSmooth only controls spatial blur in RenderMaskBlur.

commit.message:
Add dynamic AI sentence generation with Hinglish support

Replace static 10-sentence bank with on-demand AI generation via Claude API.
Sentences use Hinglish (Hindi + English mix) for accessibility. Includes
combinatorial mock template engine (2000+ unique sentences), dedup system
with hash + Jaccard similarity, retry logic with static data fallback,
and generating phase UI with spinner/error states.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

rewrite(compress): switch to Ghostscript — drop pdf-lib entirely

pdf-lib is a pure-JavaScript PDF serialiser. Saving a 33 MB document
re-serialises every object in the file and blocks the Node.js event loop
for 5–10 minutes. That is the root cause of the 10-minute hang.

New approach: Ghostscript (native C++, installed in Dockerfile).
  - Handles CMYK/RGB/Gray images natively
  - Downsamples images to target DPI using Bicubic filter
  - -dConvertCMYKImagesToRGB reduces 4-ch CMYK → 3-ch sRGB (~25% bonus)
  - Runs in 60–120 s on a 33 MB scanned PDF (vs 5–10 min with pdf-lib)
  - Async-job pattern means no HTTP timeout regardless of duration

Level settings:
  low    200 DPI, QFactor 0.3  → ~30-50% reduction
  medium 150 DPI, QFactor 0.6  → ~50-70% reduction
  high   100 DPI, QFactor 1.2  → ~70-85% reduction

Also: update processing message to 'takes 1–2 minutes' (honest UX).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

uifont_ali_update: fix [PDF:Subset] qualifier format for enhanced font mode

Three bugs prevented REPORTS_ENHANCED_FONTHANDLING=yes from embedding fonts:

1. Static font entries (Courier, Helvetica, Arial, Times, Tahoma, Verdana)
   used _subset_line() which generated entries without the ".." qualifier
   required by Oracle Reports enhanced font handler. Replaced with
   _subset_line_q() calls using proper qualifier-based format:
     "Courier"..     = LiberationMono-Regular   (was: "Courier" = ...)
     "Courier New".. = LiberationMono-Regular   (was: "Courier New" = ...)
   Most-specific entries (BoldItalic, Bold, Italic) come first; ".." last.

2. _style_to_qualifier() returned empty string for Regular/Book/Plain,
   producing entries without ".." for custom fonts ("Corp Rg" = Corp_Rg).
   Oracle Reports enhanced mode ignores these; now returns ".." for Regular
   so every [PDF:Subset] entry uses the required qualifier format.

3. PS-name alias generation compared fam_nospace (family without spaces) to
   psbase, which was always equal for "Corp Rg" / "CorpRg", preventing alias
   generation. Changed to compare the full family string to psbase so families
   with spaces ("Corp Rg") correctly generate PS-name aliases ("CorpRg").

Diagnosis confirmed: .rdf stores fonts as "Corp Rg" and "Courier New"
(with spaces, Windows font family names). uifont.ali entries existed but
lacked ".." so Oracle Reports enhanced font handler silently ignored them.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v1.0.5: NSIS installer, folder picker on first run, open-folder button

- Switch Windows target from portable → nsis + zip. NSIS creates a
  proper installer with Start Menu/desktop shortcuts and an uninstaller;
  eliminates the self-extracting portable exe that Windows Defender was
  treating as suspicious.
- First-run dialog (Electron dialog API) asks user to pick their project
  folder before anything else starts. Choice is saved in Electron userData
  (AppData\Roaming\…\prefs.json) and reused on every subsequent launch.
- "Change…" button in footer lets users switch project folders at any
  time; app relaunches automatically into the new location.
- "📁 Open" button in footer opens the project folder in Explorer/Finder
  via shell.openPath so users can easily access their layers and builds.
- server.js now deferred until USER_DATA_DIR env var is set, so all
  bootstrap mkdirSync calls write to the user-chosen folder.
- Add author/copyright metadata; update appId to com.tojiba.nftcompositor2.

Fix Figure 8-2 SVG slopes and Q12 answer key

Graph lines had wrong slopes (D: P=15-3.75Q, S: P=3.75Q) placing
pre-tax equilibrium at Q=2 — same as post-tax quantity. Corrected to
D: P=12-1.5Q, S: P=1.5Q so pre-tax eq is at Q=4, P=$6 and post-tax
at Q=2 (decrease of 2). Also fixed Q12 answer from "increases by $3"
to "decreases by $3" — a tax always reduces seller price.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

All references updated: /shabby-apartment-it/ → /it/casa-dei-sogni/

commit.message:
fix: Isolate circuit breaker per asset type, resolve stuck events, fix SQ→XYZ ticker

Circuit breaker was shared between crypto and stocks — stock losses ($-42 PnL)
triggered a 24h cooldown that permanently blocked all crypto trading (0 crypto
trades ever). Now each asset type has independent circuit breaker events.

- Add asset_type column to circuit_breaker_events table
- Filter is_in_cooldown(), record_event(), peak_balance by asset_type
- Pass asset_type='crypto' from crypto loop with crypto-only PnL/trades
- Auto-resolve stale events older than 48h on DB init (one-time migration)
- Rename SQ→XYZ in watch list (Block Inc. ticker change)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: parent-child inscription tools (#254)

* feat: add parent-child inscription transaction builder

Implement builder functions for parent-child inscriptions per the Ordinals
provenance spec. The owner of a parent inscription can create child
inscriptions, establishing on-chain provenance.

- deriveChildRevealScript: derives Taproot reveal script with parent tag (tag 3)
- buildChildCommitTransaction: commit tx with fee estimation for extra parent
  input and parent return output in the reveal
- buildChildRevealTransaction: 2-input/2-output reveal tx (commit script-path +
  parent key-path → parent return dust + child inscription)
- lookupParentInscription: Xverse API lookup for parent UTXO location

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add parent-child inscription MCP tools

Register 3 new MCP tools for creating child inscriptions:

- estimate_child_inscription_fee: calculates cost including extra parent UTXO
  input and parent return output overhead
- inscribe_child (step 1): validates parent ownership via Xverse API, builds
  and broadcasts commit tx funded from cardinal UTXOs
- inscribe_child_reveal (step 2): signs reveal with btcPrivateKey (script-path)
  and taprootPrivateKey (key-path), returns parent to owner, creates child

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: register child inscription tools in tool index

Import and call registerChildInscriptionTools in registerAllTools(),
placed after registerOrdinalsTools() in the Layer 1 Bitcoin section.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
